div1.entryFree: Swagger, to rant, to be noisy and boisterous: what hempen homespuns have we --ing here? Mids. III, 1, 79. will he s. himself out on's own eyes? Troil. V, 2, 136. the --ing upspring, Hml. I, 4, 9. Especially == to bluster, to bully: a terrible oath, with a --ing accent sharply twanged off, Tw. III, 4, 197. when I came to wive, . . . by --ing could I never thrive, V, 408. hang him, --ing rascal! H4B II, 4, 76. H4B II, 4, 76 H4B II, 4, 76 H4B II, 4, 76 H4B II, 4, 76 113 (very imperfectly understood by Mrs Quickly). an I should have been --ed out of my life, Lr. IV, 6, 243 (Edgar, in the character of a peasant: zwaggered). squabble, s., swear, Oth. II, 3, 281. With before the person bullied: he'll not s. with a Barbary hen, H4B II, 4, 107. a rascal that --ed with me last night, H5 IV, 7, 131.
